task_completion.description: The agent loads the sequence of astronomical images into AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The AstroImageJ main window is visible displaying an astronomical image or image stack (likely showing stars on a black background). The window title or file dialog may reference the WASP-12 dataset.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the target star (WASP-12) and appropriate comparison stars for differential photometry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Colored aperture rings (usually green for target, red for comparison) are visible around multiple stars in the image display window.

task_completion.description: The agent generates and displays a light curve plot showing the star's flux over time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window (e.g., 'Multi-Plot Main') is visible showing data points (Flux vs. Time/JD). The data should show a distinct 'U' shaped dip indicating a transit.

task_completion.description: The agent fits a model or trend line to the data to characterize the transit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solid line (often red or blue) is overlaid on the scatter plot data in the plotting window, or a 'Fit Panel' dialog is open showing fit parameters.

task_completion.description: The agent reports the measured transit depth, mid-transit time, and duration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent produces text output or points to a UI panel showing: Transit Depth ~1.4% (or ~14 ppt/0.014), Duration ~2.7 hours, and a Mid-transit time.

task_completion.description: The agent calculates and reports the planet's radius in Jupiter radii.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent reports a planet radius value approximately between 1.7 and 1.9 R_jup (derived from the 1.599 R_sun host star radius and ~1.4% depth).

integrity.description: The agent uses the AstroImageJ Graphical User Interface (GUI) for analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard Java-based AstroImageJ interface (menus, toolbars, plot windows), not a Python script or terminal-only approach.

integrity.description: The light curve is derived from the actual images, not simulated or hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plotted light curve contains noise/scatter typical of ground-based observation, not a perfect theoretical curve. The data points correspond to the loaded image sequence.

integrity.description: The agent derives values from the data rather than looking up WASP-12b properties online.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent performs the photometry and fitting steps before reporting values. It does not simply state 'WASP-12b has a radius of 1.79 R_jup' without showing the analysis work.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: WASP-12b. Expected Transit Depth: ~1.4% (0.014 relative flux). Expected Duration: ~2.7 hours. Expected Planet Radius: ~1.79 Jupiter radii (calculation: sqrt(0.014) * 1.599 R_sun * conversion factors). The dataset is real ground-based imagery from Jan 5-6, 2016, so the light curve will have noise but the transit dip should be clearly visible.
